title: Real-time Patient Queue Dashboard
description: The system shall display each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time in real-time on a live dashboard.
rationale: To enable triage nurses or physicians to monitor the queue status in real-time, improving efficiency and patient care coordination.
business_value: Enhances operational efficiency by allowing healthcare professionals to quickly assess and manage patient flow, leading to better resource utilization and faster patient outcomes.

source.description: As a triage nurse or physician I need a live dashboard that displays each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time So that I can monitor the queue status in real-time

acceptance_criteria: I see each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time
preconditions: The system has real-time data updates enabled
Patients are already triaged and assigned an urgency level
Estimated wait times are calculated based on current queue and resource availability
postconditions: Each patient's urgency level is displayed on the dashboard in real time
An estimated wait time for each patient is displayed on the dashboard in real time
functional_constraints: The system must update the dashboard every 5 seconds or less to ensure real-time data
security_requirements: Access to the live dashboard should be restricted to authorized triage nurses and physicians
Data transmitted between the server and client should be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must handle a minimum of 100 patients simultaneously without degradation in performance
non_functional_requirements.availability: The dashboard must be available at least 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to scale up to support an increase of 500 patients simultaneously
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, providing guidance on how to resolve issues

edge_cases: What happens if a patient's urgency level changes while on the dashboard?
How does the system handle network disruptions that could affect real-time data updates?
What if there are no patients in the queue, should the dashboard still be functional?
How does the estimated wait time change as new patients arrive and others are treated?
dependencies: Authentication and authorization system for users

interfaces.name: Patient Data API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to fetch real-time patient data, including urgency levels and wait times.

interfaces.name: Live Dashboard Interface
interfaces.description: This interface is where the requirement is implemented, providing a visual display of patient urgency levels and estimated wait times.

test_cases.description: Verify that the dashboard updates every 5 seconds with new data when a patient's status changes.
test_cases.expected_result: The dashboard should refresh within 5 seconds and display updated urgency levels and wait times.

test_cases.description: Test the system under high load with 150 patients to ensure it remains responsive.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should handle up to 150 patients simultaneously without performance degradation.
